pbContactData.summary: Precision machining shop owner. Boeing background. NTMA member.

identity.fitReason: CNC machining shop, likely high air demand, Mesa AZ metro

rapport_starters: NTMA member — ask about the chapter
Aerospace work = tight tolerances, air quality matters
intel_nuggets: Runs a CNC machining shop — number of machines unknown, probably 3-6 based on revenue.
Revenue ~$2M (SalesNav data from 6 months ago — may have changed).
Does some aerospace work — unclear if AS9100 certified or just aerospace-adjacent.
Current compressor situation unknown — no data in CRM.
rapport.opening_line: Hey Mike, I noticed you run a machining shop out in Mesa — I work with a lot of shops like yours on their compressed air setups.
rapport.adapted_script: You know he runs CNC machines but not how many or which brands. Your discovery has to fill those gaps — ask about his equipment, his air usage, and his current compressor. Listen for specific machine names (Haas, Mazak, etc.) and watch LiveAnalysis for detections. The revenue data is 6 months old and the employee count is a range — verify during the call. If he mentions aerospace, dig into whether it's AS9100 (changes the dryer recommendation from refrigerated to desiccant). Don't assume the compressor is broken — he may not be actively shopping. Find the pain first.
watch_outs: Revenue and employee data may be stale — verify, don't cite
Aerospace work doesn't necessarily mean AS9100 — ask before assuming
He's a Boeing-trained machinist — don't dumb things down
No prior interaction data — this is a true cold call
product_reference: JRS-5E — 5HP, 18 CFM @ 150 PSI, 230V/1ph, $4,995 (if smaller shop than expected)
JDD-40 — Desiccant dryer, -60°F dewpoint, $7,495 (if AS9100 confirmed)

interactions.summary: Opened VSD rotary screw compressor email 1x
